content.problemStatement:
Given a string SSS and a character XXX as input, print the first position of the character in the string if it is present.


Input Format

The first line contains a string SSS.
The second line contains a single character XXX.


Output Format

Print the **first position** (0-based index) of the character in the string if it is present.
Print "**-1**" if the character is not present in the string.

code.Python3:
# Read the input string
input_string = input()

# Read the character to search for
search_char = input()

# Initialize a variable to store the position of the character
position = -1

# Use a for loop to search for the character in the string
for i in range(len(input_string)):
    if input_string[i] == search_char:
        position = i
        break

# Print the result
print(position)
